article_title: KDE Plasma 6.7 Ships Per-Screen Virtual Desktops After 21 Years, Revives the Oxygen Theme, and Lets HDR and ICC Color Coexist
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: KDE Plasma 6.7 Ships Per-Screen Virtual Desktops After 21 Years, Revives the Oxygen Theme, and Lets HDR and ICC Color Coexist
content_preview.summary: KDE released Plasma 6.7 on June 16, 2026, adding independent virtual desktops per monitor, a refreshed KDE 4-era Oxygen theme, and simultaneous HDR and ICC color management.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The KDE community released Plasma 6.7, the newest version of its open-source desktop environment, on June 16, 2026, [according to Linuxiac](https://linuxiac.com/kde-plasma-6-7-desktop-environment-released/). The headline change is a feature users had requested for two decades: the ability to run independent virtual desktops on each connected monitor. The release also revives the Oxygen theme that shipped by default in KDE 4 and removes a long-standing limitation that forced users to...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (~700 words, within the 400-1200 range). Standard Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Technical depth is appropriate and accessible. KDE Plasma, Oxygen, KWin, Wayland, Breeze, and Union are all spelled verbatim (Rule 7 satisfied).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk and sha256-verified against manifest (all match). source-0 (kde.org, OFF-allowlist official primary, status 200): all four block quotes confirmed VERBATIM — 'After 21 years, hopefully it will have been worth the wait... per-screen virtual desktops have finally arrived!'; the Oxygen 'back up to par with the current Breeze theme' quote; the ICC/HDR 'now both are possible at the same time!' quote; and 'Plasma 6.7 marks the inaugural release of Union, here with a tech preview'. Release date 'Tuesday, 16 June 2026' confirmed. Air/Horos wallpapers, push-to-talk, Background Apps, Discover Install button, and rounded Breeze list/grid highlights all confirmed. source-1 (linuxiac.com, ALLOWLISTED, status 200): corroborates every load-bearing fact independently — June 16 2026 release (carries the lead), per-screen virtual desktops, Oxygen+Air revival with adaptive opacity and panel positions, simultaneous ICC+HDR for compatible displays, CPU-rendered/fullscreen/integrated-Intel-GPU performance, Union CSS theming for Plasma/QtQuick/QtWidgets as a QtQuick tech preview disabled by default, six maintenance updates before Plasma 6.8 planned for mid-October, and Breeze rounded list/grid highlights. source-2 (phoronix.com, ALLOWLISTED, status 200): the captured snapshot is the full short Larabel news item; it confirms per-screen virtual desktops, the 'Wayland-only' direction, Discover polish, Air-as-light-Oxygen, and Union's debut. However, the THREE specific claims the article attributes to Phoronix are NOT present in the captured snapshot: (1) the per-screen feature 'does not function on X11 or XWayland'; (2) 'KWin now supports the Wayland session management protocol, which lets applications remember their sizes and positions after a restart'; (3) the multi-GPU 'GPU lacking OpenGL acceleration no longer prevents other GPUs from providing 3D acceleration'. None of these three specifics appear in any of the three snapshots (kde.org and Linuxiac contain no X11/XWayland/session-management/multi-GPU/OpenGL/3D-acceleration text either). The general 'Wayland-only' framing IS supported by Phoronix ('gears up for Plasma 6.8 in going Wayland-only').
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline, summary, and Overview lead are fully sourced and accurate: the marquee features (per-screen virtual desktops, Oxygen revival, HDR+ICC coexistence) and the June 16 2026 date are all corroborated by the allowlisted Linuxiac source and by verbatim kde.org quotes. Internal cross-reference to the GNOME 50 article (/article/2026-03/18-gnome-50-ships-as-the-first-major-desktop-environment-to-fully-remove-x11-...) resolves to an existing published article. The only accuracy gap is the three Phoronix-attributed body specifics that the cited Phoronix snapshot does not state (see source_verification) — these are plausible real Plasma 6.7 features but are unsupported by the captured sources as cited.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing. The Analysis section frames KDE's Wayland-first trajectory factually against GNOME's completed X11 removal without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Plasma 6.7 coverage in the archive. Related but distinct from the GNOME 50 (2026-03) and Ubuntu 26.04 (2026-04) Linux-desktop pieces; not a duplicate.
concerns: OFF-ALLOWLIST PRIMARY: kde.org (the official announcement) is not on config/source_allowlist.txt. It is the Rule 9 official primary source; every load-bearing fact cited to it is independently corroborated by the allowlisted Linuxiac source, so its use is acceptable but is documented in the corrections record. The allowlist was NOT modified.

PHORONIX MISATTRIBUTION: three body specifics (Wayland/X11/XWayland limitation, KWin Wayland session-management protocol, multi-GPU OpenGL/3D-acceleration) are attributed to Phoronix but do not appear in the captured Phoronix snapshot (nor in the other two sources). These are non-lead subordinate claims; the headline, summary, and lead do not rest on Phoronix. Recoverable via a single corrections entry, so APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than REJECT.
recommendations: File the corrections record documenting (a) the off-allowlist kde.org primary and (b) the three Phoronix-attributed specifics absent from the cited Phoronix article.

Future submissions should attribute Wayland/KWin/multi-GPU technical specifics only to a source whose captured snapshot actually contains them, or cite the kde.org changelog directly for those details.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong, well-sourced News piece whose headline, summary, lead, and all four direct quotes are verbatim-accurate and corroborated by the allowlisted Linuxiac source. Two recoverable issues — an off-allowlist (but corroborated) kde.org primary and three Phoronix-attributed body specifics absent from the cited Phoronix snapshot — are honestly summarizable in a corrections note and do not touch the headline/summary/lead. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
